58	set_option autoImplicit false
59	set_option linter.style.setOption false
60	
61	universe u
62	
63	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
70	
71	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
72	
73	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
74	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
75	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
76	
77	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
78	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
80	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
81	
82	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
83	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
84	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
85	
86	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
87	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
88	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
89	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
90	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
91	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
92	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
93	    Algebra kbar
94	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
95	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
96	
97	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
98	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
99	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
100	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
101	
102	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
103	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
104	irreducibility instances. -/
105	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
106	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
107	
108	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
109	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
110	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
111	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
112	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
113	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
114	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
115	
116	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
117	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
118	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
119	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
120	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
121	
122	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
123	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
124	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
125	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
126	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
127	
128	The chain:
129	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
130	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
131	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
132	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
133	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
134	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
135	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
136	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
137	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
138	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
139	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
140	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
141	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
142	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
143	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
144	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
145	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
146	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
147	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
148	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
149	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
150	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
151	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
152	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
153	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
154	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
155	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
156	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
157	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
158	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
159	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
160	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
161	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
162	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
163	    · intro x y h
164	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
165	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
166	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
167	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
168	      apply Subtype.ext
169	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
170	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
171	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
172	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
173	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
174	      exact key
175	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
176	  -- proper.
177	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
178	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
179	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
180	    exact hbij
181	  infer_instance
182	
183	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
184	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
185	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
186	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
187	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
188	  sorry
189	
190	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
191	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
192	acceptable for iter-165). -/
193	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
194	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
195	  sorry
196	
197	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
198	
199	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
200	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
201	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
202	
203	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
204	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
205	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
206	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
207	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
208	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
209	  let f : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
210	  let m : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
211	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (m := m) f
212	    (fun i ↦ by
213	      fin_cases i <;> simp [f, m, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
214	        MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X])
215	    (fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;> simp [m])
216	    (by
217	      classical
218	      intro p hp
219	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
220	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
221	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
222	        have := hp
223	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
224	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
225	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
226	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
227	        exact this
228	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
229	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
230	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
231	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
232	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
233	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
234	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
235	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
236	        by_contra h
237	        push Not at h
238	        apply hd_ne
239	        ext k
240	        fin_cases k
241	        · simpa using h.1
242	        · simpa using h.2
243	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
244	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
245	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
246	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
247	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
248	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
249	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
250	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
251	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
252	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
253	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
254	
255	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
256	
257	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
258	private def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
259	  | 0 => 1
260	  | 1 => 0
261	
262	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
263	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
264	
265	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
266	  fin_cases i <;> decide
267	
268	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
269	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
270	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
271	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
272	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
273	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
274	
275	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
276	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
277	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
278	
279	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
280	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
281	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
282	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
283	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
284	
285	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
286	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
287	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
288	
289	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
290	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
291	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
292	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
293	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
294	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
295	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
296	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
297	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
298	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
299	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
300	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
301	
302	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
303	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
304	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
305	    kbar →+*
306	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
307	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
308	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
309	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
310	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
311	
312	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
313	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
314	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
315	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
316	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
317	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
318	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
319	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
320	    (fun _ : Unit =>
321	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
322	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
323	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
324	
325	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
326	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
327	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
328	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
329	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
330	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
331	  · intro r
332	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
333	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
334	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
335	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
336	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
337	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
338	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
339	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
340	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
341	      MvPolynomial.C r
342	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
343	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
344	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
345	  · intro _
346	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
347	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
348	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
349	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
350	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
351	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
352	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
353	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
354	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
355	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
356	      MvPolynomial.X ()
357	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
358	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
359	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
360	
361	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
362	
363	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
364	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
365	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
366	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
367	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
368	
369	Proof structure (iter-172):
370	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
371	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
372	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
373	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
374	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
375	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
376	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
377	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
378	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
379	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
380	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
381	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
382	  classical
383	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
384	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
385	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
386	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
387	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
388	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
389	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
390	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
391	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
392	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
393	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
394	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
395	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
396	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
397	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
398	    apply top_unique
399	    intro p _
400	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
401	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
402	      intro r
403	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
404	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
405	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
406	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
407	      rw [← h]
408	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
409	    · -- add case
410	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
411	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
412	    · -- mul_X case
413	      intro p₁ j hp₁
414	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
415	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
416	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
417	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
418	    v hx dv hxd
419	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
420	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
421	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
422	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
423	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
424	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
425	    apply Subtype.ext
426	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
427	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
428	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
429	    exact key
430	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
431	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
432	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
433	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
434	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
435	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
436	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
437	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
438	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
439	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
440	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
441	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
442	      have h := hai
443	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
444	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
445	      omega
446	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
447	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
448	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
449	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
450	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
451	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
452	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
453	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
454	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
455	    simp only [pow_one]
456	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
457	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
458	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
459	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
460	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
461	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
462	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
463	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
464	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
465	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
466	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
467	      subst heq_i
468	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
469	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
470	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
471	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
472	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
473	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
474	      subst heq_i
475	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
476	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
477	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
478	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
479	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
480	  intro y
481	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
482	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
483	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
484	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
485	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
486	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
487	    rw [htop]; trivial
488	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
489	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
490	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
491	  · -- mem case
492	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
493	    have hgen :=
494	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
495	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
496	    rw [hgen]
497	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
498	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
499	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
500	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
501	    rw [map_pow, hX]
502	  · -- algebraMap case
503	    intro r
504	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
505	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
506	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
507	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
508	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
509	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
510	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
511	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
512	    rw [hr₀]
513	    rfl
514	  · -- add case
515	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
516	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
517	  · -- mul case
518	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
519	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
520	
521	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
522	
523	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
524	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
525	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
526	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
527	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
528	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
529	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
530	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
531	      RingHom.id _ := by
532	  ext x
533	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
534	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
535	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
536	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
537	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
538	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
539	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
540	
541	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
542	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
543	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
544	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
545	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
546	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
547	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
548	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
549	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
550	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
551	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
552	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
553	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
554	
555	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
556	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
557	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
558	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
559	
560	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
561	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
562	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
563	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
564	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
565	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
566	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
567	        (algebraMap kbar
568	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
569	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
570	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
571	  ext r
572	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
573	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
574	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
575	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
576	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
577	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
578	      algebraMap kbar
579	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
580	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
581	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
582	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
583	    rfl
584	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
585	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
586	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
587	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
588	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
589	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
590	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
591	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
592	  rw [← hinv, hround]
593	  rfl
594	
595	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
596	
597	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
598	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
599	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
600	
601	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
602	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
603	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
604	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
605	
606	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
607	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
608	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
609	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
610	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
611	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
612	
613	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
614	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
615	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
616	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
617	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
618	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
619	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
620	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
621	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
622	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
623	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
624	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
625	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
626	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
627	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
628	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
629	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
630	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
631	    exact hi.map _
632	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
633	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
634	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
635	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
636	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
637	
638	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
639	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
640	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
641	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
642	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
643	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
644	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
645	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
646	  Over.homMk
647	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
648	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
649	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
650	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
651	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
652	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
653	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
654	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
655	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
656	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
657	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
658	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
659	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
660	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
661	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
662	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
663	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
664	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
665	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
666	      ext r
667	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
668	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
669	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
670	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
671	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
672	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
673	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
674	
675	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
676	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
677	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
678	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
679	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
680	
681	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
682	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
683	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
684	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
685	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
686	
687	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
688	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
689	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
690	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
691	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
692	
693	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
694	
695	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
696	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
697	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
698	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
699	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))

700	
701	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
702	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
703	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
704	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
705	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
706	
707	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
708	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
709	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
710	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
711	
712	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
713	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
714	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
715	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
716	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
717	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
718	
719	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
720	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
721	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
722	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
723	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
724	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
725	
726	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
727	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
728	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
729	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
730	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
731	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
732	
733	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
734	
735	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
736	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
737	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
738	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
739	
740	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
741	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
742	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
743	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
744	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
745	
746	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
747	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
748	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
749	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
750	
751	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
752	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
753	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
754	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
755	
756	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
757	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
758	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
759	
760	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
761	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
762	element is finitely presented), bridged through
763	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
764	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
765	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
766	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
767	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
768	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
769	      inferInstance)
770	
771	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
772	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
773	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
774	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
775	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
776	
777	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
778	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
779	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
780	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
781	
782	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
783	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
784	  unfold GmRing
785	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
786	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
787	
788	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
789	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
790	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
791	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
792	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
793	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
794	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
795	  infer_instance
796	
797	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
798	
799	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
800	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
801	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
802	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
803	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
804	
805	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
806	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
807	`W = Gm`). -/
808	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
809	
810	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
811	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
812	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
813	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
814	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
815	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
816	
817	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
818	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
819	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
820	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
821	  η[Gm kbar]
822	
823	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
824	
825	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
826	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
827	definition). -/
828	
829	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
830	homogeneous degree-`1` element `f`.
831	
832	Generic in the element `f` so we can apply it to either `(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart
833	input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`) or `X i` (after the
834	`Matrix.cons_val` reduction). A pure rewrite chasing `awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` +
835	the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
836	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
837	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1) :
838	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf Nat.one_pos ≫
839	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
840	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
841	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
842	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
843	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
844	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
845	  rfl
846	
847	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
848	
849	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
850	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
851	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
852	
853	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
854	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
855	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
856	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
857	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
858	
859	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
860	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
861	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
862	
863	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
864	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
865	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
866	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
867	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
868	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
869	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
870	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
871	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
872	
873	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
874	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
875	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
876	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
877	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
878	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
879	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
880	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
881	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
882	        GmRing kbar))
883	
884	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
885	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
886	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
887	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
888	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
889	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
890	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
891	
892	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is `Spec ((Away 𝒜 X_i) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
893	
894	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
895	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
896	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
897	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
898	
899	The case split on `i : Fin 2` is needed because `(projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i`
900	reduces to `Proj.awayι _ ((![X 0, X 1]) i) _ _`, and `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` is not
901	definitional — it holds via `Matrix.cons_val_zero`/`_one` after `fin_cases`. -/
902	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
903	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
904	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
905	        (TensorProduct kbar
906	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
907	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
908	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
909	  -- The starting form is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) ((cover).openCover.f i)`,
910	  -- where `(cover).openCover.f i` unfolds to `Proj.awayι _ ((![X 0, X 1]) i) _ _`. We need
911	  -- the `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` reduction, which is `rfl` for concrete `i = 0, 1` but not
912	  -- generically. So we pattern-match on `i`.
913	  match i with
914	  | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
915	    pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
916	      pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
917	      pullback.congrHom
918	        (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (MvPolynomial.X 0)
919	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0)) rfl ≪≫
920	      pullbackSpecIso kbar
921	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
922	          (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
923	        (GmRing kbar)
924	  | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
925	    pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
926	      pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
927	      pullback.congrHom
928	        (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (MvPolynomial.X 1)
929	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1)) rfl ≪≫
930	      pullbackSpecIso kbar
931	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
932	          (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
933	        (GmRing kbar)
934	
935	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
936	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
937	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
938	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
939	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
940	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
941	
942	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
943	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
944	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso

988	7. `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst`: swap fst ↔ snd.

993	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
994	  -- Strategy:
995	  -- LHS = iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (chart_map) ≫ Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom
996	  --     → iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (chart_map) ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)   [awayι_comp_PLB_hom]

1000	  --       pullback.congrHom.hom ≫ pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)  [pullbackSpecIso_hom_base]

1012	              (fun _ : Unit =>
1013	                (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem

1026	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))) := by

1028	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _) ≫
1029	      Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)

1052	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp

1240	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
1241	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
1242	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
1243	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-$0$ curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean
4	
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	
10	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
11	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
12	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
13	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
14	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
15	% keystone directly). The headline thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
16	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
17	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
18	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
19	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
20	
21	The genus-$0$ arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
22	($J = \Spec k$); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
23	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$
24	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
25	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field $k$, so the
26	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
27	O(-2)) = 0$, hence $df = 0$, hence $f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
28	characteristic-$p$ Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant $f$ can have $df = 0$. The
29	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
30	
31	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
32	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
33	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
34	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
35	derives the genus-$0$ base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
36	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
37	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
38	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that $\mathbb P^1$ is
39	\emph{already complete} and $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
40	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
41	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut} (the project's
42	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
43	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
44	\mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (the M\"obius scaling fixing both $0$ and
45	$\infty$), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
46	G_m$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising $f(0) = \eta_A$ and composing $h := \sigma_\times
47	\fatsemi f$, one applies additivity of $\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
48	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
49	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ proper, $W = \mathbb G_m$, and
50	base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$. Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of
51	scaling, the $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)$ is
52	\emph{constant} at $\eta_A$, so the decomposition collapses to $h = p \fatsemi f$, i.e.\
53	$f(\lambda x) = f(x)$. Specialising $x = 1$ gives that $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant, and since
54	$\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ with $A$ separated, $f$ is constant
55	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
56	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
57	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
58	$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$, and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
59	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical $\mathbb G_a$-additive
60	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility
61	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
62	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$ critical path. The corollary that a
63	pointed regular map of abelian
64	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
65	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-$0$ path; Milne's
66	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
67	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-$0$ critical path
68	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
69	gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant
70	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-$0$
71	curves (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}, $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$) yields the
72	headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
73	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
74	neither Serre duality nor the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is
75	fully decoupled from Route~A.
76	
77	\medskip
78	
79	Throughout, $\bar k$ is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
80	\emph{abelian variety} over $\bar k$ is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
81	project's Lean encoding $A$ is an object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ carrying
82	$\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, and $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$.
83	
84	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
85	
86	\begin{lemma}
87	
88	\leanok
89	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
90	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
92	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
93	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let $X$ be a complete variety, $Y$ and $Z$
94	  % any varieties, and $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ a morphism such that for some
95	  % $y_0 \in Y$, $f(X \times \{y_0\})$ is a single point $z_0$ of $Z$. Then there is a
96	  % morphism $g \colon Y \to Z$ such that if $p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y$ is the
97	  % projection, $f = g \circ p_2$."
98	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
99	  Let $X$ be a complete variety, and let $Y$, $Z$ be any varieties over $\bar k$. Let
100	  $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ be a morphism such that for some $y_0 \in Y$ the image
101	  $f(X \times \{y_0\})$ is a single point $z_0 \in Z$. Then there is a morphism
102	  $g \colon Y \to Z$ with $f = g \circ p_2$, where $p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y$ is the
103	  projection; that is, $f$ depends only on the $Y$-coordinate, and $g(y) = f(x_0, y)$ for any
104	  fixed $x_0 \in X$.
105	\end{lemma}
106	
107	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point $x_0 \in X$, and define $g \colon Y \to Z$ by
108	% $g(y) = f(x_0, y)$. Since $X \times Y$ is a variety, to show that $f = g \circ p_2$, it is
109	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of $X \times Y$. Let
110	% $U$ be an affine open neighbourhood of $z_0$ in $Z$, $F = Z - U$, and $G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))$;
111	% then $G$ is closed in $Y$ since $X$ is complete and hence $p_2$ is a closed map. Further
112	% $y_0 \notin G$ since $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}$. Therefore $Y - G = V$ is a non-empty
113	% open subset of $Y$. For each $y \in V$, the complete variety $X \times \{y\}$ gets mapped by
114	% $f$ into the affine variety $U$, and hence to a single point of $U$. But this means that for
115	% any $x \in X$, $y \in V$, $f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)$, and this proves our
116	% assertion."
117	\begin{proof}
118	
119	\uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
120	\leanok
121	% NOTE (iter-159 review): the iter-157 unsoundness REMAINS REPAIRED. The collapse hypothesis
122	% (_hf : f(X x {y0}) = {z0}) is threaded through all three helpers (rigidity_lemma ->
123	% rigidity_core -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open) and GENUINELY CONSUMED in the body of
124	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open (proves y0 \notin G, the load-bearing non-emptiness of V := Y - G).
125	% The counterexample f = fst is excluded (under f = fst, _hf forces X to collapse to a point).
126	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open is sorry-free in its OWN body (hfib, the pullback-fibre fact over the
127	% k-bar-point y0, is closed). rigidity_snd_lift and snd_left_isClosedMap are axiom-clean (no sorryAx).
128	% NOTE (iter-160): the \uses edges run FORWARD along the true Lean dependency order
129	% (rigidity_lemma -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open -> rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine), so the
130	% not-proven status of the residual sorry propagates up and de-launders BOTH thm:rigidity_lemma and
131	% lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open. No backward edge from the leaf lemmas back up the chain.
132	% NOTE (iter-161): bridge 2 (rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine) was DECOMPOSED into two named
133	% top-level sub-lemmas along the route-B (cohomology-free) split:
134	%   - Step 2, morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints: "two morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a
135	%     reduced Jacobson source into a separated target are equal" -- PROVEN axiom-clean (the
136	%     dense-closed-points coproduct probe + ext_of_isDominant; the connective Mathlib lacks).
137	%   - Step 1, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine: Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy
138	%     -- the genuinely-deep residual, still `sorry`. THIS is now the chain's lone deep residual
139	%     (bridge 2's body is real assembly: Step 2 proven, the JacobsonSpace U instance now derivable).
140	% SIGNATURE CHANGE (iter-161, plan-authorized): Step 2's globalisation needs the closed points of
141	% the saturated open U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson, which over Spec k-bar follows from (X tensor Y)
142	% being LOCALLY OF FINITE TYPE. So [LocallyOfFiniteType (X tensor Y).hom] is now carried on FIVE
143	% chain lemmas (rigidity_lemma, rigidity_core, rigidity_eqOn_dense_open,
144	% rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine), in
145	% addition to [IsAlgClosed kbar]. Free downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type
146	% over k-bar. The earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim is hereby retired.
147	
148	  Fix any point $x_0 \in X$ and define $g \colon Y \to Z$ by $g(y) = f(x_0, y)$ (the
149	  composite of $y \mapsto (x_0, y)$ with $f$). Since $X \times Y$ is a variety (in
150	  particular reduced and irreducible, so separated and with no embedded structure), to prove
151	  the equality of morphisms $f = g \circ p_2$ it suffices to prove they agree on a non-empty
152	  open subset of $X \times Y$.
153	
154	  Let $U$ be an affine open neighbourhood of $z_0$ in $Z$, put $F = Z - U$ (a closed set),
155	  and set $G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))$. Because $X$ is complete, the projection
156	  $p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y$ is a \emph{closed} map (this is the defining property of
157	  completeness/properness); hence $G$, the image of the closed set $f^{-1}(F)$, is closed in
158	  $Y$. The hypothesis $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U$ gives
159	  $f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing$, so $y_0 \notin G$. Therefore
160	  $V := Y - G$ is a non-empty open subset of $Y$.
161	
162	  For each $y \in V$ we have $X \times \{y\} \cap f^{-1}(F) = \varnothing$, i.e.
163	  $f(X \times \{y\}) \subseteq U$. Thus the complete (proper) variety $X \times \{y\}$ is
164	  mapped by $f$ into the \emph{affine} variety $U$. A proper connected variety mapping to an
165	  affine variety has image a single point (a global regular function on a complete connected
166	  variety is constant). Hence $f(X \times \{y\})$ is one point, necessarily
167	  $f(x_0, y) = g(y)$. So for all $x \in X$ and $y \in V$,
168	  \[ f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g(y) = (g \circ p_2)(x, y). \]
169	  The two morphisms $f$ and $g \circ p_2$ thus agree on the non-empty open
170	  $X \times V$, which is dense in the irreducible $X \times Y$; as $Z$ is separated they
171	  agree everywhere. \qedhere
172	\end{proof}
173	
174	\begin{remark}
175	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_cube_free}
176	  The proof of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} uses \emph{only} (i) that completeness of $X$ makes
177	  $p_2$ a closed map, and (ii) that a proper connected variety has no nonconstant map to an
178	  affine variety. It uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, and is valid in
179	  arbitrary characteristic. This is the designated formalisation entry point: it is the
180	  lowest-prerequisite link of the chain. (Mumford gives this as the geometric heart of the
181	  ``second proof'' of commutativity of an abelian variety; Milne packages the same statement
182	  as the Rigidity Theorem~1.1, requiring $V$ complete and $V \times W$ geometrically
183	  irreducible.)
184	\end{remark}
185	
186	\begin{remark}
187	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_decomposition}
188	  The Lean formalisation of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} splits into three helper steps, mirroring
189	  the proof above: \texttt{rigidity\_snd\_lift} (the cartesian-monoidal algebra identifying the
190	  collapsed map $(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$ followed by $f$ with $g \circ p_2$);
191	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} (the scheme-level gluing: two maps out of the geometrically irreducible
192	  reduced $X \times Y$ into the separated $Z$ that agree on a non-empty open subset agree
193	  everywhere, by separatedness); and \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open}
194	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}, the genuine geometry: the construction of the non-empty
195	  open $X \times V$ and the slice-constancy of $f$ on it). The collapse hypothesis
196	  $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}$ must be threaded through \emph{all three} helpers, since it is
197	  what makes $V$ non-empty; a decomposition that drops it from the lower helpers is unsound
198	  (see \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}).
199	
200	  \emph{Status (iter-161).} Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) is \textbf{built} as the axiom-clean
201	  helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap}. The fibre-over-$y_0$ fact is \textbf{closed} (via the
202	  coarse \texttt{PullbackCarrier} layer), so \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open} is
203	  \texttt{sorry}-free in its own body. Bridge~2 (slice-constancy on the saturated open $U$,
204	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}) has now been \textbf{decomposed} along its
205	  cohomology-free route-(c) split into two named sub-lemmas: the globalisation
206	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2, ``agree at every closed point of a reduced
207	  Jacobson source $\Rightarrow$ equal'') is \textbf{proven axiom-clean}, and the per-closed-slice
208	  constancy \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine} (Step~1, Mumford's slice
209	  argument) is the chain's \emph{single genuinely-deep residual} \texttt{sorry}. The body of
210	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} is therefore real assembly (Step~2 over Step~1).
211	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) STALE as of iter-162 — Step~1 is now PROVEN and the WHOLE
212	  % Rigidity-Lemma chain (thm:rigidity_lemma … lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine,
213	  % via the new helper lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral) is sorry-free + axiom-clean
214	  % ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}, re-verified). The "single genuinely-deep residual
215	  % sorry" wording here and in the proof blocks of lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open / …saturated… /
216	  % …eqAt_closedPoint… should be refreshed by the plan/blueprint-writer to "chain closed iter-162".
217	
218	  This route requires the base field to be algebraically closed \emph{and} the source to be of finite
219	  type: Step~2 needs the closed points of $U$ to be dense (the Jacobson-space property), which over
220	  the algebraically closed $\bar k$ holds because $X \times Y$ is locally of finite type.
221	  Accordingly the formalization carries \textbf{both} $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and
222	  $[\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]$ across the chain lemmas
223	  (\texttt{rigidity\_lemma}, \texttt{rigidity\_core}, \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open},
224	  \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_saturated\_open\_to\_affine},
225	  \texttt{rigidity\_eqAt\_closedPoint\_of\_proper\_into\_affine}); the earlier claim that
226	  $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ is the \emph{only} added instance is retired. Both cost nothing
227	  downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over the algebraically closed $\bar k$,
228	  and the consumers already assume it. The relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
229	  $f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O$ framing is a genuine Mathlib cohomology gap and is \emph{deliberately
230	  avoided}.
231	\end{remark}

233	\begin{lemma}
234	
235	\leanok
236	[Dense-open agreement: the geometric heart of the Rigidity Lemma]
237	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
238	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
239	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
240	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let $X$ be a complete variety, $Y$ and $Z$
241	  % any varieties, and $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ a morphism such that for some
242	  % $y_0 \in Y$, $f(X \times \{y_0\})$ is a single point $z_0$ of $Z$. Then there is a
243	  % morphism $g \colon Y \to Z$ such that if $p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y$ is the
244	  % projection, $f = g \circ p_2$."
245	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
246	  dense-open construction is the body of that proof).}
247	  Let $X$ be a complete variety and $Y$, $Z$ any varieties over $\bar k$. Let
248	  $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ be a morphism, fix a point $x_0 \in X$, and suppose given
249	  $y_0 \in Y$, $z_0 \in Z$ with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}
250	  \[ f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \qquad
251	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
252	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
253	  Then there is a non-empty open subset $U \subseteq X \times Y$ on which $f$ agrees, as a
254	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map $\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f$, where
255	  $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
256	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)$ is the ``$(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$'' endomorphism. Concretely
257	  $U = X \times V$ with $V := Y - G$, $G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))$ for an affine open
258	  neighbourhood $U_0$ of $z_0$, and on $U$ one has $f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)$.
259	
260	  \medskip
261	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of $V$ is exactly
262	  the assertion $y_0 \notin G$, and this holds \emph{because} $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
263	  \subseteq U_0$ keeps the slice over $y_0$ inside the affine $U_0$, away from $F = Z - U_0$. A
264	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for $X = Y = Z$ and
265	  $f = p_1$ the first projection, $f$ and $\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0$
266	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in $\{x = x_0\}$, which has empty
267	  interior in the irreducible $X \times Y$). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
268	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
269	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.
270	
271	  \medskip
272	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
273	  source.} The prose already takes $\bar k$ algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
274	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
275	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field $\kappa(y)$ to be $\bar k$ itself, so that a
276	  proper integral slice has global sections $\Gamma = \bar k$ and maps to a single $\bar k$-point of
277	  the affine $U_0$; this requires $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$. Second, the globalisation of the
278	  per-slice agreement (Step~2 of the bridge, \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}) needs the
279	  closed points of the saturated open $U$ to be \emph{dense} --- i.e.\ $U$ to be a Jacobson space.
280	  Over the algebraically closed $\bar k$ this holds precisely because $X \times Y$ is \emph{locally of
281	  finite type} over $\bar k$ (\texttt{LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace} transports the Jacobson
282	  property of $\Spec \bar k$, and \texttt{closure\_closedPoints} then gives density); this requires
283	  $[\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]$. Accordingly the formalization carries
284	  \emph{both} $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and
285	  $[\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]$ on this lemma (in addition to
286	  $[\mathtt{Field}\ \bar k]$), and propagates the same two instances to \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma},
287	  its core helper \texttt{rigidity\_core}, and the bridge sub-lemmas
288	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} and
289	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}. Both cost nothing downstream: the
290	  consumers \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} and \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (Lean
291	  \texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}) \emph{already} assume
292	  $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ --- the base-field variable is literally named $\mathtt{kbar}$ ---
293	  and curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over $\bar k$, so the
294	  locally-of-finite-type instance is automatic there.
295	\end{lemma}
